product_overview: Meme Battle Arena is a web app where users create accounts, upload meme images, and compete in head-to-head voting battles to improve their memes’ ratings. Users can also review their uploaded memes and view the overall leaderboard.
core_goals: Create and access a user account
Upload memes with titles for participation in the arena
Vote on meme matchups to influence ratings

features.name: User Registration
features.description: Allows a new user to create an account using a display name, email, and password.
user_flows: Navigate to /register -> Fill in display name -> Fill in email -> Fill in password -> Submit the registration form -> See account created and return to the home page
Navigate to /register -> Fill in display name -> Fill in email -> Fill in a password shorter than 8 characters -> Submit the registration form -> See inline validation feedback

features.name: User Login
features.description: Allows an existing user to sign in with email and password.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Fill in email -> Fill in password -> Submit the login form -> See account access granted and return to the home page
Navigate to /login -> Fill in email -> Fill in password -> Submit the login form with invalid credentials -> See inline error feedback

features.name: Session Persistence and Logout
features.description: Keeps a signed-in user available across reloads and lets them end their session from the app.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in -> Reload the page -> See the signed-in state remain available
Navigate to / -> Sign in -> Wait for the session to expire during normal use -> Continue using the app without interruption
Navigate to / -> Sign in -> Log out -> Try to open a protected page -> See redirect to /login

features.name: Protected Route Access
features.description: Prevents unauthenticated users from opening protected areas of the app.
user_flows: Navigate to /upload while logged out -> See redirect to /login
Navigate to /my-memes while logged out -> See redirect to /login
Navigate to /arena while logged out -> See redirect to /login
Navigate to /leaderboard while logged out -> See redirect to /login

features.name: Meme Upload
features.description: Lets a signed-in user add a meme image with a title for later voting and ranking.
user_flows: Navigate to /upload -> Drag and drop an image file or browse for one -> See the selected image preview -> Fill in a title -> Submit the upload -> See upload progress and return to /my-memes on success
Navigate to /upload -> Drag and drop a file with a disallowed type -> See inline file type feedback
Navigate to /upload -> Select an image larger than 5MB -> See inline file size feedback
Navigate to /upload -> Select an image -> Leave the title too long -> See inline title validation feedback

features.name: My Memes Management
features.description: Shows the user’s uploaded memes and allows them to remove any meme they own.
user_flows: Navigate to /my-memes -> View a grid of uploaded memes with ratings and win-loss stats
Navigate to /my-memes -> Choose a meme to delete -> Confirm the browser dialog -> See the meme removed from the grid

features.name: Arena Voting
features.description: Presents head-to-head meme matchups so the user can vote for a winner and advance through battles.
user_flows: Navigate to /arena -> See two memes displayed side by side -> Choose one meme to vote for or use the left/right arrow keys -> See the chosen meme highlighted immediately -> See the reveal animation with updated ratings and point change -> Wait for the next matchup to load automatically
Navigate to /arena -> See fewer than two memes available -> See the empty state prompting the user to upload more memes

features.name: Leaderboard Viewing
features.description: Displays all active memes sorted by rating and highlights the user’s own uploads.
user_flows: Navigate to /leaderboard -> View memes ranked from highest rating to lowest rating -> See each row show rank, title, thumbnail, rating, wins, and losses
Navigate to /leaderboard -> View the table -> See the user’s own uploaded memes visually highlighted

routes.description: Home page; demo content plus an auth-aware status block (login link or logged-in user + nav links)
Upload a meme image with title, drag-and-drop, client-side validation, and progress bar
Grid of the logged-in user's own uploaded memes, each with a delete button
Head-to-head meme voting screen with keyboard and click voting, reveal animation, auto-advance
Ranked table of all active memes by rating, with the current user's own memes highlighted

features.name: User Registration
features.description: Create a new account with email, password, and display name
user_interactions: Fill display name input
Fill email input
Fill password input (min 8 characters)
Click "Create account" to submit
See inline error on failure (e.g. email already taken)
Redirected to / on success

features.name: User Login
features.description: Log in with email and password, receive an access token and an HTTP-only refresh cookie
user_interactions: Fill email input field
Fill password input field
Click "Log in" button to submit
See inline error message on invalid credentials
Redirected to / on success

features.name: Session persistence and silent token refresh
features.description: Access token is mirrored to encrypted local storage and rehydrated on reload; an expired access token triggers a single-flight silent refresh via the HTTP-only refresh cookie before retrying the original request
user_interactions: Reload the page while logged in and remain logged in
Continue using the app uninterrupted when an access token silently expires

features.name: Logout
features.description: Revoke the refresh token server-side and clear all local/persisted auth and feature state
user_interactions: Click "Log out" button
Redirected/blocked from protected pages afterward

features.name: Route protection
features.description: Protected pages rehydrate the session from storage, validate the token's local expiry, and redirect to /login if there is no valid session
user_interactions: Visit /upload, /my-memes, /arena, or /leaderboard while logged out
Get redirected to /login

features.name: Meme Upload
features.description: Upload a meme image with a title via drag-and-drop or file picker, with client-side type/size pre-validation and an upload progress bar
user_interactions: Drag and drop an image file onto the dropzone, or click to browse
See a live preview of the selected image
Fill in a title (max 100 characters)
See an inline error for disallowed file types or files over 5MB
Click "Upload" and watch the progress bar
Redirected to /my-memes on success

features.name: My Memes list and delete
features.description: View all memes uploaded by the current user and delete any of them
user_interactions: View a grid of uploaded memes with rating/wins/losses
Click "Delete" on a meme card
Confirm the browser confirm() dialog
See the meme disappear from the grid immediately

features.name: Arena — matchup voting
features.description: Vote on a server-dealt head-to-head matchup between two memes; ratings update via ELO after each vote
user_interactions: See two memes dealt side by side
Click a meme card to vote for it, or press the Left/Right arrow keys
See the selected card highlighted immediately (optimistic UI)
See the reveal animation with old/new ratings and the point delta
Automatically advance to a new matchup ~1.5 seconds after the reveal
See an "upload more memes" empty state if fewer than 2 memes exist

features.name: Leaderboard
features.description: Ranked list of all active memes sorted by rating, highlighting the current user's own entries
user_interactions: View memes ranked by rating, highest first
See rank, title, thumbnail, rating, wins, and losses per row
See own uploaded memes visually highlighted in the table

known_limitations.issue: No shared (app) route-group shell with header/nav
known_limitations.impact: Each protected page (upload, my-memes, arena, leaderboard) wraps itself in AuthGuard individually rather than sharing one layout/nav; navigation links currently only live inline in the home page's AuthStatus component

known_limitations.issue: window.confirm used instead of a real confirm-dialog widget
known_limitations.impact: Meme deletion confirmation is a native browser dialog, not a styled in-app modal
Success/error feedback is shown as plain inline text rather than a toast
Scaffolded per the standard project layout but no form currently needs conditional Tailwind class merging
